Prompt: Write a concise, friendly overview about . Keep it factual and verifiable, with a warm, conversational tone—like a well‑traveled friend. Present the most meaningful points first. Weave information about naturally into the prose and omit . Surround all names of people with and all location names with . Do not use headings, titles, bullet points, or any paragraph labels (for example: 'History:', 'Geography:', 'Food:', 'Culture:'). Avoid starting any line with a topic name followed by a colon. Use short, natural paragraphs. When you refer to the place, you don't need to repeat the state or country.
PromptSuffix: If relevant, add one short paragraph that naturally contrasts <place> with <previous_place>. Keep the same friendly, flowing paragraph style—no headings, bullet points, or paragraph labels (for example: 'Differences:' or topic names). Do not repeat facts already covered in: <previous_answer>.
SharingPromptTemplate: Create a brief, factual description (5-7 sentences) of this road trip route. Include the starting and ending locations, key stops along the way, and relevant geographical or logistical details. Keep it informative and straightforward. Route details: {ROUTE_DETAILS} Example style: "This route travels from [first place] to [last place], covering [distance/region]. The journey includes stops at [key locations] and passes through [geographical features or notable areas]. Total travel spans [time period/distance] across [type of terrain/region]."
